collocations.cn: 盼望做某事（to 后接动名词）

collocations.en: look forward to hearing from you

patterns.title: look forward to + 动名词
patterns.explain: look forward to 中的 to 是介词，后面接动词 -ing，不能接动词原形。
examples: I look forward to seeing you.（我盼望见到你。）
We look forward to hearing from you soon.

collocations.cn: 不得不 / 必须做某事

patterns.title: have to vs must
patterns.explain: have to 强调客观需要（外界要求），must 强调主观义务或强烈判断；否定 don't have to 表「不必」，mustn't 表「禁止」。
examples: I have to get up early tomorrow.（明天我得早起。）
You don't have to come if you're busy.（忙的话不必来。）

patterns.title: had to（过去式）
patterns.explain: have to 的过去式为 had to，表示过去某时客观上必须做某事。
examples: I had to wait for an hour.（我不得不等了一小时。）
She had to sell her car.（她不得不把车卖掉。）

patterns.title: be used to + 动名词
patterns.explain: be used to 表示「习惯于」，to 是介词，后接 -ing；区别于 used to do（过去常常）。
examples: I am used to getting up early.
She is used to living alone.

collocations.cn: 过去常常做某事（现在不了）

collocations.en: didn't use to / didn't used to

patterns.title: used to + 动词原形
patterns.explain: used to do 表示过去习惯性的动作或状态，暗含「现在已不再如此」。易混：be used to doing = 习惯于。
examples: He used to work in a bank.（他以前在银行工作。）
I didn't use to like coffee.（我过去不喜欢咖啡。）

collocations.en: lend sb sth = lend sth to sb
Can you lend me your dictionary?
collocations.cn: 你能把词典借我一下吗？

patterns.title: lend 与 borrow 的区别
patterns.explain: lend = 借出（主语是出借方）；borrow = 借入（主语是借入方）。lend sb sth / lend sth to sb；borrow sth from sb。
examples: Can you lend me your pen?（能把笔借我吗？）
May I borrow your umbrella?（我能借你的伞吗？）

patterns.title: borrow 与 lend 的区别
patterns.explain: borrow 的方向是「借入」，from 指出借方；lend 的方向是「借出」。
examples: Can I borrow your pen for a minute?
He borrowed money from a friend.

patterns.title: afford 的用法
patterns.explain: afford 后接名词或 to do，常与 can/could/can't 连用，表示经济上是否承担得起。
examples: We cannot afford such an expensive car.
I can't afford to travel abroad this year.

patterns.title: refuse to do
patterns.explain: refuse 后接不定式，不接动名词；表示拒绝做某事。
examples: He refused to answer my question.
She refused to lend him any money.

patterns.title: expect 的用法
patterns.explain: expect 后可接名词、that 从句、或 sb to do；进行式 be expecting 常指等待访客或重要消息。
examples: We are expecting some friends tonight.
I expect he will arrive soon.

collocations.en: not allow sb to do sth

patterns.title: allow sb to do / be allowed to do
patterns.explain: allow 后接宾语 + 不定式；被动式 be allowed to 表示「被允许」。
examples: My parents don't allow me to stay up late.
You are not allowed to park here.

patterns.title: spend + 时间/金钱 + on / (in) doing
patterns.explain: spend 的主语是人；on 后接名词，(in) doing 后接动名词表示花时间做某事。
examples: I spent two hours on my homework.
She spends too much time watching TV.

patterns.title: waste + 时间 + (in) doing
patterns.explain: waste 与 spend 结构类似，但含「白白浪费」之意。
examples: Watching TV all day is a waste of time.
Don't waste time arguing.

patterns.title: complain about + 名词/动名词
patterns.explain: complain 是不及物动词，about 后接抱怨的内容；进行时 always complaining 可表反复抱怨、含不满语气。
examples: He complained about the rain almost every day.
He is always complaining about the weather.

patterns.title: except vs besides
patterns.explain: except = 不包括在内（「除了……之外，其他都……」）；besides = 包括在内（「除了……还有」）。
examples: Everyone went out except him.（除了他，大家都出去了。）
He thought of everything except the weather.

collocations.en: neither of + 复数名词/代词

patterns.title: neither of / neither ... nor
patterns.explain: neither of 后接复数名词或 them/us 等，动词可用单数或复数；neither...nor 连接两个并列成分。
examples: Neither of the answers is correct.
He could not understand me, and neither could I understand him.

patterns.title: mind + 动名词
patterns.explain: mind 后接动名词，不接不定式；Would you mind doing...? 是礼貌请求句式。
examples: Would you mind opening the window?
I don't mind waiting.

patterns.title: enjoy + 动名词
patterns.explain: enjoy 后必须接动名词，不能接 to do。
examples: I enjoy reading in the evening.
They enjoyed themselves at the party.

patterns.title: keep + doing
patterns.explain: keep doing 表示持续或反复做某事；keep + adj 表示保持状态。
examples: Keep trying!
She kept silent during the meeting.

patterns.title: enough ... to ...
patterns.explain: enough 修饰形容词/副词时放后面（old enough）；修饰名词时放前面（enough money）。enough to do 表「足够做某事」。
examples: He is old enough to go to school.
We don't have enough coins for the machine.

patterns.title: too ... to ...
patterns.explain: too...to 表示「太……以至于不能……」，to 后接动词原形，含否定意义。
examples: He is too young to go to school.
The box is too heavy to carry.

patterns.title: none of one's business
patterns.explain: 固定表达，表示「不关你的事」，常用于拒绝回答私人问题。
examples: How I spend my money is my own business.
It's none of your business.（不关你的事。）

patterns.title: reply vs answer
patterns.explain: reply 是不及物动词，须加 to：reply to sb；answer 可作及物动词：answer sb / answer a question。
examples: She replied to my question with a smile.
The doctor replied that there was nothing to worry about.

collocations.cn: 放弃做某事 / 戒除某习惯

patterns.title: give up + 动名词
patterns.explain: give up 后接动名词，表示放弃某事或戒除习惯。
examples: He decided to give up smoking last year.
He has given up smoking at last.

patterns.title: set out / take part in
patterns.explain: set out 表示出发、启程；set out to do 表示着手做某事。
examples: The swimmer will set out from the French coast at dawn.
They set out to climb the mountain.

patterns.title: look for vs look forward to
patterns.explain: look for = 寻找；look forward to = 盼望，to 后接动名词。二者含义完全不同，勿混淆。
examples: I am looking for my keys.
I look forward to seeing you.

patterns.title: call at vs call on
patterns.explain: call at + 地点（到某处去）；call on + 人（拜访某人）。
examples: The letter asked him to call at the station that afternoon.

patterns.title: stay up vs get up
patterns.explain: stay up = 熬夜、不睡；get up = 起床。allow sb to stay up late = 允许某人熬夜。
examples: Can I stay up tonight?
My parents don't allow me to stay up late.

collocations.en: It's one's turn to do sth

patterns.title: It's one's turn to do
patterns.explain: 固定句型，表示按顺序轮到自己做某事。
examples: It's my turn to wash the dishes.
Whose turn is it to speak?

patterns.title: pay for / ask sb for
patterns.explain: pay for + 物品；ask sb for + 物品，表示向某人索取。
examples: How much did you pay for the book?
He asked the shop assistant for help.

patterns.title: dream of / think of + 动名词
patterns.explain: dream of/about 与 think of 后接动名词，表示梦想、考虑做某事。
examples: She has always dreamed of visiting Paris.
He dreamed of a quiet house with a garden.

patterns.title: be determined to do
patterns.explain: determined 是形容词，与 be 连用，后接不定式，表示坚定的决心。
examples: She is determined to finish the race.
They were determined to succeed.

patterns.title: be proud of
patterns.explain: proud 是形容词，与 be 连用，of 后接名词或动名词。
examples: His parents are proud of him.
She is proud of her work.

patterns.title: belong to
patterns.explain: belong 是不及物动词，to 后接所有者或组织，不能用于被动语态。
examples: This book belongs to me.
Does this coat belong to you?
